translations.en: Then al-Hurr b. Yazid spurred his horse and crossed to the camp of al-Husayn (peace be upon him), with his hand upon his head saying: 'O Allah! I repent to You — forgive me, for I have terrified the hearts of Your friends and the children of Your Prophet. O son of the Messenger of Allah! Is there repentance for me?' He said: 'Yes. Allah has accepted your repentance.' He said: 'Do you permit me to fight on your behalf?' He granted him permission. He went out saying: 'I will strike your necks with the sword, for the best one who dwelt in the land of al-Khayf.' He killed eighteen men, then was killed. Al-Husayn came to him and said: 'Bravo, O Hurr! You are truly free, just as you were named, in this world and the next.' Then al-Husayn recited [verse in praise of al-Hurr]. Then Zuhayr b. al-Qayn al-Bajali set out, saying: 'Today we shall meet your grandfather the Prophet, and Hasan, and 'Ali.' He killed nineteen, then was struck down. Then Habib b. Mathahir al-Asadi set out, saying: 'We are more pristine than you — we support the best of people.' He killed thirty-one, then was killed. Then 'Abdullah b. Abi 'Urwa al-Ghifari set out and killed twenty, then was killed. Then Burayr b. Khudayr — the finest Quran reciter of his time — set out and killed thirty, then was killed. Then Malik b. Anas al-Kahili set out, saying: 'The clan of 'Ali are the Shi'a of the Beneficent, and the clan of Harb are the Shi'a of Satan.' He killed eighteen, then was killed. Then Ziyad b. Mahasir al-Kindi set out and killed nine, then was killed. Then Wahb b. Wahb — a Christian who had converted at the hands of al-Husayn along with his mother — fought and killed seven or eight, then was captured and beheaded by order of 'Umar b. Sa'd. His head was thrown toward al-Husayn's camp. His mother took his sword. Al-Husayn said to her: 'O Umm Wahb! Sit, for Allah has lifted jihad from women. You and your son are with my grandfather in Paradise.' Then Hilal b. Hajjaj set out and killed thirteen, then was killed. Then 'Abdullah b. Muslim b. 'Aqil set out and killed three, then was killed. Then 'Ali b. al-Husayn al-Asghar set out. Al-Husayn wept and said: 'O Allah! Be the witness.' He killed ten, then returned saying: 'O father! Thirst!' Al-Husayn said: 'Be patient, my son — your grandfather will give you to drink from the most fulfilling cup.' He returned and killed forty-four, then was killed. Then al-Qasim b. al-Hasan b. 'Ali set out, saying: 'Do not grieve — today you will attain Paradise.' He killed three, then was thrown from his horse.
